GG.detail: Homozygous wild-type (Arg/Arg). Normal lymphoid-specific phosphatase (LYP) function. Proper T-cell receptor signaling threshold. Lowest PTPN22-attributable autoimmune risk.

AG.detail: Heterozygous (Arg/Trp). Gain-of-function LYP variant leads to hyperactive phosphatase, suppressing T-cell receptor signaling and impairing thymic negative selection. Associated with ~1.5–2× increased risk of rheumatoid arthritis, type 1 diabetes, systemic lupus erythematosus, autoimmune thyroid disease, and vitiligo. One of the most replicated autoimmune susceptibility alleles outside the HLA region.

AA.summary: High autoimmune risk — homozygous 620W
AA.detail: Homozygous Trp/Trp. Extremely rare genotype (A allele frequency ~0.07 globally, primarily European). Substantially elevated risk for multiple autoimmune conditions. Paradoxically, LYP is a gain-of-function variant that reduces T-cell activation threshold, allowing autoreactive T cells to escape thymic deletion. Family history of autoimmune disease should prompt heightened surveillance.

sources.name: Bottini et al. 2004 — PTPN22 and type 1 diabetes (Nature Genetics)

description: IFNL3/IL28B (rs12979860) — interferon lambda 3 upstream variant; the C allele is strongly associated with spontaneous and treatment-induced hepatitis C virus clearance, one of the most important pharmacogenomic markers in hepatology

CC.summary: Best HCV treatment response — high IFNL3 expression
CC.detail: Homozygous favorable allele. Highest interferon-lambda 3 expression. Best sustained virological response (SVR) rates to pegylated interferon-alfa and ribavirin therapy (~80% for genotype 1 HCV). Also higher spontaneous HCV clearance rate (~50% vs ~20% for TT). In the DAA (direct-acting antiviral) era, this genotype retains prognostic value for treatment-naïve patients and shortened regimen eligibility.

CT.detail: Heterozygous. Intermediate IFNL3 expression. SVR rates approximately 50–60% with interferon-based therapy for genotype 1 HCV. Intermediate spontaneous clearance probability. With modern DAAs, SVR rates approach CC levels, but pre-treatment viral kinetics are slower.

TT.summary: Reduced HCV clearance — lower IFNL3 expression
TT.detail: Homozygous unfavorable allele. Lowest interferon-lambda 3 expression. SVR rates approximately 25–30% with interferon-based therapy for genotype 1 HCV. Lowest spontaneous HCV clearance rate (~20%). More likely to develop chronic infection after acute exposure. Modern DAAs substantially mitigate this disadvantage, but treatment duration or regimen choice may still be influenced. The T allele is more common in African-descent populations (~0.50) contributing to observed disparities in HCV treatment outcomes.

sources.name: Ge et al. 2009 — IL28B and HCV treatment response (Nature)

description: COL1A1 Sp1 binding site (rs1800012) — intronic variant in collagen type I alpha 1 chain; the A allele increases COL1A1 transcription, altering collagen I α1/α2 ratio and reducing bone mineral density

CC.summary: Normal collagen I ratio — standard bone density
CC.detail: Homozygous wild-type. Normal Sp1 transcription factor binding at the COL1A1 promoter. Balanced α1(I)/α2(I) collagen chain ratio. Standard bone mineral density for age and sex. No COL1A1-attributable fracture risk increase.

AC.detail: Heterozygous. Enhanced Sp1 binding increases COL1A1 transcription, mildly skewing the α1(I)/α2(I) ratio. Modestly reduced bone mineral density (~0.2–0.3 SD lower at the lumbar spine in meta-analyses). Fracture risk increased approximately 1.3× compared to CC. Adequate calcium, vitamin D, and weight-bearing exercise are recommended.

AA.summary: Reduced bone density — increased fracture risk
AA.detail: Homozygous risk allele. Substantially increased COL1A1 transcription. Significantly altered collagen I stoichiometry producing homotrimeric α1(I)₃ collagen with reduced mechanical strength. Bone mineral density reduced ~0.5 SD. Fracture risk approximately 1.8–2× compared to CC, particularly vertebral fractures. Rare genotype (~2% in Europeans). DXA screening and osteoporosis prevention strategies should be considered early.

sources.name: Mann et al. 2001 — COL1A1 Sp1 and osteoporosis meta-analysis (J Clin Invest)

description: ADRB1 Arg389Gly (rs1801253) — beta-1 adrenergic receptor variant in the intracellular signaling domain; the C allele (Arg389) has higher receptor activity and greater response to beta-blocker therapy

CC.summary: Higher β1-receptor activity — best beta-blocker response
CC.detail: Homozygous Arg389 (major allele). Higher basal and agonist-stimulated adenylyl cyclase activity. Greater cardiac contractile response to catecholamines. Best blood pressure and heart rate response to beta-blocker therapy (metoprolol, atenolol, bisoprolol). Associated with better outcomes in heart failure trials of beta-blockers (BEST, MERIT-HF subanalyses).

CG.detail: Heterozygous Arg/Gly. Intermediate adenylyl cyclase coupling. Moderate response to beta-blockers. Blood pressure reduction and heart rate response between CC and GG. Most clinical trials show benefit from standard beta-blocker dosing.

GG.summary: Reduced β1-receptor activity — attenuated beta-blocker response
GG.detail: Homozygous Gly389. Reduced G-protein coupling efficiency. Lower basal cardiac adrenergic tone. Diminished blood pressure–lowering response to beta-blockers in some pharmacogenomic studies. May benefit less from beta-blocker monotherapy for hypertension. Alternative or combination antihypertensive strategies may be preferable. Gly389 frequency is ~0.30 globally.

sources.name: Johnson et al. 2003 — ADRB1 Arg389Gly and beta-blocker response (Clin Pharmacol Ther)

description: TYR R402Q (rs1126809) — tyrosinase variant affecting the copper-binding domain; the A allele (Gln402) reduces melanin synthesis, contributing to lighter skin, lighter eyes, and increased melanoma susceptibility

GG.detail: Homozygous wild-type (Arg/Arg). Full tyrosinase enzymatic activity in melanocytes. Normal melanin biosynthesis. Darker baseline pigmentation. Lowest TYR-attributable melanoma risk.

AG.summary: Mildly reduced pigmentation — modest melanoma risk
AG.detail: Heterozygous (Arg/Gln). Mildly reduced tyrosinase activity. Slightly lighter skin and eye pigmentation. Modestly increased UV sensitivity and melanoma risk. The A allele is common in Europeans (~0.18) and contributes to normal pigmentation variation. When homozygous and combined with other TYR variants, can contribute to oculocutaneous albinism type 1B.

AA.summary: Reduced tyrosinase — lighter pigmentation, higher melanoma risk
AA.detail: Homozygous Gln/Gln. Substantially reduced tyrosinase activity. Lighter skin and eye color. Higher UV sensitivity. Increased melanoma risk (~1.3–1.5× compared to GG). In combination with other pigmentation gene variants (MC1R, OCA2), contributes to very fair skin phenotype. Sun protection strategies are particularly important.

sources.name: Gudbjartsson et al. 2008 — TYR variants and pigmentation (Nature Genetics)

description: FTO intron 1 variant (rs9930506) — fat mass and obesity-associated gene; the G allele is associated with increased BMI, obesity risk, and type 2 diabetes via altered IRX3/IRX5 expression affecting adipocyte thermogenesis

AA.summary: Lower obesity risk
AA.detail: Homozygous protective allele. Normal IRX3/IRX5 expression pattern in adipocytes. Beige/brown adipocyte thermogenesis is not impaired by this locus. Lowest FTO intron 1–attributable obesity risk. Average BMI effect baseline.

AG.detail: Heterozygous. Mildly altered chromatin looping affects IRX3/IRX5 enhancer interactions. BMI increased by approximately 0.3–0.5 kg/m² per risk allele in GWAS. Modest increase in adiposity. Effect is modifiable by physical activity — active carriers show significantly attenuated BMI increase.

GG.summary: Increased obesity risk — altered adipocyte thermogenesis
GG.detail: Homozygous risk allele. Enhanced IRX3/IRX5 expression shifts white adipocytes away from thermogenic beige phenotype. BMI increased by ~0.6–1.0 kg/m² on average. Obesity risk approximately 1.3–1.7× compared to AA. Associated with increased type 2 diabetes risk (mediated through adiposity). Effect is strongly attenuated by regular physical activity. Complements rs9939609 (FTO intronic) — both are in the same LD block but tag slightly different signals.

sources.name: Frayling et al. 2007 — FTO and BMI (Science)
Claussnitzer et al. 2015 — FTO mechanism via IRX3/IRX5 (NEJM)

description: IL10 -819C>T (rs1800871) — interleukin-10 promoter variant; the C allele (minor) is associated with altered IL-10 transcription, affecting anti-inflammatory cytokine balance and susceptibility to inflammatory and autoimmune conditions

AA.summary: Higher IL-10 production — anti-inflammatory
AA.detail: Homozygous T/-819 (on forward strand A = T on the commonly reported strand). Associated with higher interleukin-10 production. Stronger anti-inflammatory cytokine response. May be protective against inflammatory and autoimmune conditions. Conversely, high IL-10 may impair pathogen clearance in some infectious disease contexts.

AC.detail: Heterozygous. Intermediate IL-10 transcription from the promoter haplotype. Balanced pro/anti-inflammatory cytokine ratio. Most common genotype in many populations.

CC.summary: Lower IL-10 production — pro-inflammatory tendency
CC.detail: Homozygous C/-819 (on commonly reported strand). Lower interleukin-10 promoter activity. Reduced anti-inflammatory capacity. Associated with increased susceptibility to systemic lupus erythematosus, inflammatory bowel disease, and Graves' disease in multiple meta-analyses. Enhanced pathogen-driven inflammation — may confer advantage against certain infections but at cost of autoimmune/inflammatory risk.

sources.name: Eskdale et al. 1998 — IL-10 promoter haplotypes and transcription
